input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable automatically)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Refresh your PATH
$env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path","Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path","User")

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover the current MAUI workload ID
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it (includes android, ios stubs, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Java (OpenJDK) — required by Android tooling

```powershell
# Discover available OpenJDK versions
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17 (required by current Android Gradle/SDK tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Refresh PATH and verify
$env:Path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path","Machine") + ";" + [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path","User")
java -version
```

## 4. Install Android SDK & accept licenses

The MAUI workload auto-installs Android SDK components on first build, but you can bootstrap explicitly:

```powershell
# Discover where the Android SDK was placed by the workload
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Fallback: check the dotnet default
if (-not (Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    $androidSdk = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK path: $androidSdk"

# If the SDK doesn't exist yet, trigger provisioning via a dummy build:
dotnet new maui -n _TempMauiProbe --force
Push-Location _TempMauiProbe
dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies `
    -f net9.0-android `
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$androidSdk" `
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
Pop-Location
Remove-Item _TempMauiProbe -Recurse -Force

# List installed Android SDK packages
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list
```

## 5. Set environment variables persistently

```powershell
# ANDROID_HOME / ANDROID_SDK_ROOT
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_SDK_ROOT", $androidSdk, "User")

# JAVA_HOME (discover dynamically)
$javaHome = (Get-Command java).Source | Split-Path | Split-Path
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $javaHome, "User")

Write-Host "ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk"
Write-Host "JAVA_HOME    = $javaHome"
```

## 6. Enable Windows App SDK / WinUI (for Windows target)

```powershell
# The MAUI workload already includes WinUI support.
# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading):
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /v AllowDevelopment /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f

# Verify Developer Mode
(Get-ItemProperty "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock").AllowDevelopment
# Should return 1
```

## 7. Create & build a test project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 8. (Optional) Set up an Android emulator

```powershell
$sdkmanager = "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List available system images to pick a version dynamically
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "system-images;android-"

# Install an image (example: API 35, x86_64)
& $sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
$avdmanager = "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch emulator
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with `net10.0` etc. if you installed a newer SDK — `dotnet workload search` always shows what matches your installed SDK.
- The `Developer Mode` reg key requires an elevated (admin) PowerShell.
- Android license acceptance (`AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true`) is a one-time step.
